title: Negative clause (P2): anti-uniform diagonalization against all finite parameter tuples

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (G4 / P2).** Prove: for **every** finite tuple $\bar a\subseteq A$ there exists $\pi\in\AUT(A)$ such that $\pi$ is **not** $\Sigma^{in}_1$-definable from the augmented parameters $\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)$.

This is the principal research content. Crucial subtlety: the parameters include $\pi(\bar a)$ (the images), which is a *gift to the adversary* — adding $2|\bar a|$ named points may 'spend' gadget addresses. The construction must ensure that naming $\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)$ still leaves infinitely many gadgets unaddressed, and that on those unaddressed gadgets $\pi$ encodes a non-$\Sigma^{in}_1$ (e.g. non-c.e.-on-a-cone) amount of information.

Suggested route:
1. Fix $X\subseteq\N$ non-c.e. relative to the cone.
2. Build $\pi$ acting on gadgets of index above $\max$ of all gadget-indices touched by $\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)$, swapping the twin pair in $G_n$ iff $n\in X$.
3. Show that a $\Sigma^{in}_1$-definition of $\pi$ from $\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)$ would yield a c.e.-on-a-cone description of $X$ (via the AKMS direction of Theorem 2.3), contradicting non-c.e.-ness of $X$.

If direct coding is fragile under the $\pi(\bar a)$ handicap, escalate to a finite-injury/forcing construction diagonalizing against the $e$-th candidate family of existential formulas.
